questions.question: What is the primary purpose of paper prototyping in the UI/UX design process?
options: A) To create a visually appealing design
B) To test the usability of the design
C) To create a functional prototype
D) To gather feedback from stakeholders
questions.explanation: Paper prototyping is used to test the usability and gather feedback from users before moving to more advanced prototypes.

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of a low-fidelity prototype?
options: A) Highly detailed and realistic
B) Simple and rough sketches
C) Colorful and interactive
D) Highly functional and tested
questions.explanation: Low-fidelity prototypes are simple, rough sketches or wireframes that are used to test the overall layout and user flow.

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of creating a functional prototype?
options: A) To test the usability and gather feedback from users
B) To create a visually appealing design
C) To gain a deeper understanding of the system's architecture
D) To test the technical feasibility of the design
questions.explanation: Creating a functional prototype allows developers to test the technical feasibility of the design and identify potential technical issues early on.

questions.question: Which of the following is a key aspect of a successful prototype?
options: A) It must be visually appealing
B) It must be highly functional
C) It must be created with the end-user in mind
D) All of the above
questions.explanation: A successful prototype should be created with the end-user in mind, be visually appealing, and be functional enough to provide valuable feedback.

questions.question: What is the main difference between a paper prototype and a digital prototype?
options: A) A paper prototype is more expensive to create
B) A digital prototype is more user-friendly
C) A paper prototype is less detailed and less interactive
D) A paper prototype is created using digital tools
questions.explanation: The main difference between a paper prototype and a digital prototype is that a paper prototype is less detailed and less interactive, while a digital prototype is more detailed and interactive.
